title: First External Reviewer Invite Pack
context: GrantLayer is publicly readable on GitHub in a developer-preview / controlled-pilot posture following GL-176 through GL-183. GL-183 defined the triage model and decided templates_deferred. This artifact prepares the controlled invite package for 2-5 external reviewers. No outreach was sent. This is docs/test/artifact only.

reviewer_profiles.name: External Backend Developer
reviewer_profiles.description: Familiar with Python, REST APIs, and SQLite or PostgreSQL. Can clone a repo, install dependencies, and run tests in approximately 10 minutes. May or may not have a grant-domain background.
key_questions: Is the quickstart clear and complete?
Does the API surface make sense?
Is the first verifiable output reproducible?

reviewer_profiles.name: AI-Agent Workflow Developer
reviewer_profiles.description: Builds or evaluates AI-agent pipelines (LangGraph, LangChain, AutoGen, or similar). Interested in audit trails, verification layers, and agentic compliance patterns.
key_questions: Does the GrantLayer integration story make sense for an AI-agent pipeline?
Is the LangGraph example legible and realistic?

reviewer_profiles.name: Grant/Compliance or Audit-Minded Reviewer
reviewer_profiles.description: Works with grant management, institutional compliance, or audit processes. Does not need to be a developer. Evaluates whether evidence bundle and audit trail concepts map to real institutional needs.
key_questions: Do the concepts (evidence bundles, audit trails, policy evaluation) reflect real institutional workflows?
Are the safety caveats about current limitations visible and appropriately cautious?

reviewer_profiles.name: Security-Minded Technical Reviewer
reviewer_profiles.description: Reviews security posture of developer-preview software. Familiar with OWASP, API security basics, and responsible disclosure practices.
key_questions: Does the repository appear free of real secrets or sensitive infrastructure paths?

invite_message_draft:
Subject: GrantLayer Developer Preview — Invitation to Review

Hello,

We are inviting a small, selected group of developers and reviewers to take an early look at GrantLayer, a technical preview / developer preview project for structured grant-application verification and AI-agent audit workflows.

Repository: https://github.com/Discodone/grantlayer.git

What GrantLayer is:
GrantLayer is a Python backend with a REST API and SDK for creating structured, verifiable evidence bundles used in grant management and agentic compliance workflows. This is a developer preview only.

What GrantLayer is not (please read before starting):
- This is not a production SaaS platform. Do not treat it as a production service.
- Tenant/workspace isolation is not yet implemented. Do not use shared or real institutional data.
- No customer onboarding process is available.
- No support SLA is offered for this developer preview.
- This is not an invitation to a paid or commercial commitment of any kind.

What we are asking of you:
1. Read README.md — can you understand what GrantLayer is in 2 minutes?
2. Read SECURITY.md — can you find the security reporting channel and current-limitations caveats?
3. Run or inspect the first verifiable output: examples/first_verifiable_output.py
4. Compare the generated output with examples/first_verifiable_output.json.
5. Note what is confusing, missing, or unclear.
6. Tell us whether the project purpose is clear in 2 minutes of reading.

Safety rules — please read before posting any feedback:
- Use synthetic/demo data only. Do not share real customer data, private grants, or institutional data in any public issue.
- Do not share secrets, credentials, API tokens, or private keys in public issues.
- Do not post exploit details publicly. If you find a potential security vulnerability, report it through GitHub Security Advisories (see SECURITY.md) — not in a public issue.
- Do not share real customer data or personally identifiable information in any public channel.

How to share feedback:
Open a public GitHub issue on the repository with your observations. For security-sensitive reports, please use GitHub Security Advisories instead of public issues.

We appreciate your time. This is a controlled review window. No commercial commitment, customer onboarding, or support guarantee is implied or offered.
reviewer_task_list: Read README.md — assess whether the project purpose is understandable within 2 minutes
Read SECURITY.md — confirm the security reporting channel and current-limitations caveats are visible and clear
Find the quickstart / first verifiable output — locate examples/first_verifiable_output.py and the quickstart instructions without guidance
Run or inspect examples/first_verifiable_output.py — attempt to run the first verifiable output example and observe whether it succeeds or fails
Compare generated output with examples/first_verifiable_output.json — determine whether the generated output matches the expected reference output
Note confusing setup steps — record any step that required guessing, searching elsewhere, or extra time
Identify broken links or stale wording — flag any hyperlink that returns 404 or documentation text that appears outdated or contradictory
Assess whether caveats are visible — evaluate whether 'not production SaaS' and 'tenant isolation not implemented' are easy to find and clearly worded
Assess whether the project purpose is understandable in 2 minutes — give a one-sentence summary of what GrantLayer does after reading only the README
feedback_questions: What did you think GrantLayer does after reading the README?
Could you find the first verifiable output quickly?
Could you run it, or at least understand what it proves without running it?
Which part of the README or documentation was hardest to follow?
Were any setup steps missing, unclear, or broken?
Are the non-production caveats clear? Were you able to find them without searching?
Are the security reporting instructions clear and easy to follow?
What would make you try the SDK or API next?
What would make you trust this project more?
What is the smallest next example you would want to see added?
reviewer_safety_instructions: Use synthetic/demo data only. The repository uses synthetic demo data throughout. All examples and reproduction steps should use synthetic identifiers (e.g. demo-subject-001, test-grant-abc). Do not use real grant application data.
Do not share secrets. Do not include API tokens, private keys, passwords, session tokens, or any value that could grant system access in public issues. Use placeholder values only.
Do not share customer data. Do not paste real grant applications, institutional records, personally identifiable information, or beneficiary data into any public channel.

Do not share private grants or institutional data. Do not include real institutional funding records, award identifiers linked to real organizations, or any non-public grant data in public issues or pull requests.

Do not post exploit details publicly. If you discover a potential security vulnerability, report it through GitHub Security Advisories as described in SECURITY.md. Do not post exploit steps, proof-of-concept payloads, or sensitive system details in public issues.

Direct security-sensitive reports to GitHub Security Advisories. If GitHub Security Advisories are unavailable for any reason, open a minimal public issue without exploit details or secrets and explicitly request a private reporting path.

feedback_recording_model.reviewer_profile: string — archetype matched (e.g. external-backend-developer, ai-agent-workflow-developer, grant-compliance-audit-reviewer, security-minded-technical-reviewer)
feedback_recording_model.public_commit_reviewed: string — git commit hash at time of review
feedback_recording_model.reviewer_tasks_attempted: array of string — tasks the reviewer attempted from the task list
feedback_recording_model.docs_clarity_score: integer 1-5 — self-reported clarity score (5 = very clear)
feedback_recording_model.trust_score: integer 1-5 — self-reported trust score (5 = high trust)
feedback_recording_model.security_feedback: string — any security-relevant observation (paraphrased, no exploit details)
feedback_recording_model.broken_links: array of string — links that returned 404 or were stale
feedback_recording_model.confusing_claims: array of string — claims that felt misleading or overclaiming
feedback_recording_model.requested_examples: array of string — examples the reviewer wanted
feedback_recording_model.severity: string — critical / high / medium / low / info
feedback_recording_model.category: string — GL-183 triage category (blocker, confusing-docs, broken-quickstart, missing-example, product-question, security-concern, production-readiness-concern, feature-request, non-scope-later)
feedback_recording_model.next_action: string — fix-now / clarify-docs / add-to-backlog / defer / close-as-non-scope / escalate-security
feedback_recording_model.follow_up_needed: boolean — true if follow-up action is required

triage_alignment.reference: GL-183 External Feedback Triage / Public Issue Hygiene

severity_levels.criteria: Serious issue affecting safety or first-use trust

non_goals: No production SaaS promise. GrantLayer is in developer-preview / controlled-pilot posture only.
No tenant/workspace isolation claim. Tenant isolation is not implemented.
No real customer data collection. Do not collect, store, or request real grant or customer data from reviewers.
No real grant/institutional data review. Reviewers must use synthetic data only.
No payment or treasury flow. Payment, wallet, and treasury features are not in current MVP scope.
No blockchain requirement in current MVP. Blockchain is planned as an optional Phase 3 layer.
No SLA or support promise. No SLA is offered for developer preview.
No public issue template creation in this issue. Templates were deferred by GL-183.
No GitHub label creation in this issue. Labels are documented only in GL-183; no API changes are made.
No reviewer outreach sent in this issue. The invite message is a draft only.

findings.recommendation: Invite 2-5 reviewers manually using the invite message draft in this pack. Do not send bulk or automated invites.

next_recommended_step: GL-185 First Reviewer Feedback Window / Feedback Capture
